description: Operating Room must provide hand hygiene facilities (handwashing sink or ABHR dispenser) within the room or at the entrance, per FGI infection control requirements.
Icu must provide hand hygiene facilities (handwashing sink or ABHR dispenser) within the room or at the entrance, per FGI infection control requirements.
Patient Room must provide hand hygiene facilities (handwashing sink or ABHR dispenser) within the room or at the entrance, per FGI infection control requirements.
Emergency must provide hand hygiene facilities (handwashing sink or ABHR dispenser) within the room or at the entrance, per FGI infection control requirements.
Sterile Core must provide hand hygiene facilities (handwashing sink or ABHR dispenser) within the room or at the entrance, per FGI infection control requirements.
Laboratory must provide hand hygiene facilities (handwashing sink or ABHR dispenser) within the room or at the entrance, per FGI infection control requirements.
Imaging must provide hand hygiene facilities (handwashing sink or ABHR dispenser) within the room or at the entrance, per FGI infection control requirements.
Pharmacy must provide hand hygiene facilities (handwashing sink or ABHR dispenser) within the room or at the entrance, per FGI infection control requirements.
Operating Room: Surgical task lighting at the surgical field. Minimum 2500 foot-candles required at the work plane.
Icu: Examination lighting at the patient bed. Minimum 100 foot-candles required at the work plane.
Patient Room: General illumination for examination and care. Minimum 50 foot-candles required at the work plane.
Emergency: Treatment and examination lighting. Minimum 200 foot-candles required at the work plane.
Nurse Station: Task lighting for documentation and medication preparation. Minimum 75 foot-candles required at the work plane.
Laboratory: Task lighting for specimen analysis. Minimum 100 foot-candles required at the work plane.
Imaging: Low ambient lighting for monitor viewing. Minimum 30 foot-candles required at the work plane.
Pharmacy: Task lighting for medication preparation. Minimum 75 foot-candles required at the work plane.
Sterile Core: Task lighting for sterile assembly. Minimum 75 foot-candles required at the work plane.
California OSHPD requires operating rooms in new construction to have a minimum clear floor area of 450 square feet (50 sqft above FGI minimum).
California OSHPD requires seismic bracing of all HVAC systems serving operating rooms and ICUs per ASCE 7 Seismic Design Category D or higher.
California OSHPD requires a minimum of 250 square feet per ICU patient room (25% above FGI minimum for new construction).
California SB 1953: All essential services buildings (hospitals) must meet OSHPD SPC-5 (highest) seismic performance standards by 2030.
citation: California SB 1953, OSHPD Seismic Compliance Program

description: California OSHPD requires a minimum 8ft-wide patient corridor in acute care hospitals, with 10ft recommended for high-traffic surgical corridors.
Texas HHSC: General operating rooms must meet FGI minimum of 400 sqft; specialty ORs (orthopedic, neurosurgery) must provide minimum 500 sqft.
Texas HHSC: ICU must be located adjacent to or within 300 feet of the emergency department and operating suite.
Texas HHSC: Emergency department must provide a minimum ratio of 150 sqft per licensed emergency bed.
Florida AHCA: Operating rooms must comply with FGI minimums; ambulatory surgery ORs must meet a minimum of 300 sqft.
Florida AHCA: Hospitals in hurricane risk zones (Zone A-C) must have generator capacity for 96 hours of full facility operation.
Florida AHCA: ICU rooms in non-hurricane-hardened buildings must have backup HVAC capable of maintaining appropriate temperatures during power outages.
Ambulatory Surgery Centers (ASC): Operating rooms must provide minimum 250 sqft for Class B procedures, 400 sqft for Class C.
Radiation oncology vaults must provide minimum 400 sqft of clear floor area with minimum 5-foot concrete shielding walls.
Psychiatric patient rooms must provide minimum 120 sqft and must have ligature-resistant fixtures and hardware throughout.
Rehabilitation facility therapy gyms must provide a minimum of 2,000 sqft or 80 sqft per patient, whichever is greater.
Long-Term Acute Care (LTAC) patient rooms must provide minimum 200 sqft private rooms with full private bathroom.
CDC/HICPAC: Operating rooms must supply HEPA-filtered air with unidirectional (laminar) airflow directly over the operating table.
citation: CDC/HICPAC Guidelines for Environmental Infection Control, 2003

description: CDC/HICPAC: Airborne Infection Isolation (AII) rooms must maintain -2.5 Pa negative pressure relative to corridor, monitored continuously with visible indicator.
citation: CDC/HICPAC Guidelines for Environmental Infection Control, Section III.A

description: CDC/HICPAC: Protective Environment (PE) rooms for immunocompromised patients must maintain +2.5 Pa positive pressure, HEPA filtration, minimum 12 ACH.
citation: CDC/HICPAC Guidelines for Environmental Infection Control, Section III.B

description: CDC/HICPAC: Hand hygiene sinks must be located within each patient room, within arm's reach of patient care activities.
CDC/HICPAC: Sterile supply must be stored in a dedicated sterile storage area with controlled temperature (60-75°F) and relative humidity (30-60%).
citation: CDC/HICPAC Guidelines for Environmental Infection Control, Section V
